div.p: This dashboard provides deeper diagnostics for failed setup actions, scheduled task issues, Splunk service activity, MSI installer messages, JSON state tracking, retry-loop prevention, force retry helper activity, marker reset activity, and raw upgrade events.
High-level troubleshooting counts for setup failures, upgrade failures, service restart activity, MSI installer errors, retry-loop prevention, state tracking issues, and installer cleanup status.

search.query: | tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count AS "Setup Error Events" FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9800, 9801, 9802, 9804, 9806, 9814, 9815, 9816, 9817) AND host IN ($hostname$)

| tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count AS "Upgrade Failure Events" FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9811, 9813) AND splunk_upgrade_events.status=Failure AND host IN ($hostname$)

| tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count AS "Service Control Events" FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9808, 9809, 9810) AND host IN ($hostname$)
| tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count AS "MSI Error Messages" FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_msi_events WHERE host IN ($hostname$)

single.title: Retry Prevention Events

search.query: | tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count AS "Retry Prevention Events" FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9819) AND host IN ($hostname$)

single.option: Same installer skipped to prevent retry loop

search.query: | tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count AS "State Tracking Errors" FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9822) AND host IN ($hostname$)
| tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count AS "Installer Cleanup Failures" FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode=9807 AND host IN ($hostname$)
| tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count AS "Installer Cleanup Success" FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9823) AND host IN ($hostname$)

div.p: Setup and precheck failures such as missing MSI installer, missing Splunk binary, failed scheduled task creation, failed scheduled task removal, failed scheduled task start, and version validation issues.

chart.title: Setup Error Trend by Event Code

search.query: | tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9800, 9801, 9802, 9804, 9806, 9814, 9815, 9816, 9817) AND host IN ($hostname$) BY splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode | rename splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode AS eventcode | eval event_description=case( eventcode=9800, "9800 - MSI Not Found", eventcode=9801, "9801 - Splunk Bin Not Found", eventcode=9802, "9802 - Failed to Create Scheduled Task", eventcode=9804, "9804 - Failed to Remove Task", eventcode=9806, "9806 - Failed to Start Task", eventcode=9814, "9814 - Failed to Parse Splunk Version", eventcode=9815, "9815 - MSI Bad Filename", eventcode=9816, "9816 - UF Version Up to Date", eventcode=9817, "9817 - Versions Too Far Apart" ) | sort - count | table event_description, count

| tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ fillnull_value="n/a" count FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9800, 9801, 9802, 9804, 9806, 9814, 9815, 9816, 9817) AND host IN ($hostname$) BY _time, splunk_upgrade_events.date, splunk_upgrade_events.time, host, splunk_upgrade_events.status, splunk_upgrade_events.message, splunk_upgrade_events.details, splunk_upgrade_events.taskname, splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode | sort 0 -_time | table splunk_upgrade_events.date, splunk_upgrade_events.time, host, splunk_upgrade_events.status, splunk_upgrade_events.message, splunk_upgrade_events.details, splunk_upgrade_events.taskname, splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode | rename splunk_upgrade_events.date AS Date, splunk_upgrade_events.time AS Time, host AS Hostname, splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode AS EventCode, splunk_upgrade_events.status AS Status, splunk_upgrade_events.message AS Message, splunk_upgrade_events.details AS Details, splunk_upgrade_events.taskname AS Taskname

div.p: Latest failed upgrade event per host with message and detail fields for remediation.

search.query: | tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ latest(_time) AS latest_time latest(splunk_upgrade_events.date) AS Date latest(splunk_upgrade_events.time) AS Time latest(splunk_upgrade_events.current_version) AS "Current UF Version" latest(splunk_upgrade_events.previous_version) AS "Previous UF Version" latest(splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode) AS EventCode latest(splunk_upgrade_events.status) AS Status latest(splunk_upgrade_events.message) AS Message latest(splunk_upgrade_events.details) AS Details FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9811, 9813) AND splunk_upgrade_events.status=Failure AND host IN ($hostname$) BY host | rename host AS Hostname | eval "Latest Event Time"=strftime(latest_time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") | table Date, Time, Hostname, "Previous UF Version", "Current UF Version", EventCode, Status, Message, Details, "Latest Event Time" | sort 0 Hostname

div.p: Service start, stop, and restart-related activity generated during the upgrade workflow.

search.query: | tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9808, 9809, 9810, 9811) AND host IN ($hostname$) BY splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode | rename splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode AS eventcode | eval event_description=case( eventcode=9808, "9808 - Splunkd Start", eventcode=9809, "9809 - Splunkd Failed to Start", eventcode=9810, "9810 - Splunkd Stopped", eventcode=9811, "9811 - Splunkd Failed to Stop" ) | sort - count | table event_description, count

| tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE host IN ($hostname$) AND splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9808, 9809, 9810, 9811) BY splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode | rename splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode AS eventcode | eval eventcode_label=case( eventcode="9808", "9808 - Splunkd service started successfully.", eventcode="9809", "9809 - Failed to start the Splunkd service.", eventcode="9810", "9810 - Splunkd service stopped successfully.", eventcode="9811", "9811 - Failed to stop the Splunkd service.", true(), eventcode . " - Unknown event code" ) | fields eventcode, eventcode_label

| tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN ($splunkd_service_action_token$) AND host IN ($hostname$) BY _time, splunk_upgrade_events.date, splunk_upgrade_events.time, host, splunk_upgrade_events.status, splunk_upgrade_events.message, splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode | sort 0 _time | table splunk_upgrade_events.date, splunk_upgrade_events.time, host, splunk_upgrade_events.status, splunk_upgrade_events.message, splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode | rename splunk_upgrade_events.date AS Date, splunk_upgrade_events.time AS Time, host AS Hostname, splunk_upgrade_events.status AS Status, splunk_upgrade_events.message AS Message, splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode AS EventCode

div.p: Windows MSI installer messages collected during the upgrade workflow.

search.query: | tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_msi_events WHERE host IN ($hostname$) BY _time, host, splunk_msi_events.date, splunk_msi_events.time, splunk_msi_events.msi_error_message | sort 0 -_time | table splunk_msi_events.date, splunk_msi_events.time, host, splunk_msi_events.msi_error_message | rename splunk_msi_events.date AS Date, splunk_msi_events.time AS Time, host AS Hostname, splunk_msi_events.msi_error_message AS "MSI Error Message"

div.h1: 6. State Management, Retry Prevention, and Retry Helper Activity
div.p: JSON state tracking, retry-loop prevention, force retry activity, helper-created retry flags, marker reset activity, and installer cleanup events used to prevent or intentionally control repeated upgrade attempts against the same MSI installer.

chart.title: State, Retry, and Helper Events Over Time

search.query: | tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9807, 9819, 9820, 9821, 9822, 9823, 9830, 9831, 9832, 9833, 9834, 9835) AND host IN ($hostname$) BY splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode | rename splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode AS eventcode | eval event_description=case( eventcode=9807, "9807 - Installer Cleanup Failed", eventcode=9819, "9819 - Retry Prevented", eventcode=9820, "9820 - Force Retry Consumed", eventcode=9821, "9821 - State File Written", eventcode=9822, "9822 - State Tracking Error", eventcode=9823, "9823 - Installer Removed", eventcode=9830, "9830 - Retry Flag Created", eventcode=9831, "9831 - Retry Helper Skipped", eventcode=9832, "9832 - Retry Helper Failed", eventcode=9833, "9833 - Retry Marker Reset", eventcode=9834, "9834 - Reset Marker Not Present", eventcode=9835, "9835 - Reset Marker Failed" ) | sort - count | table event_description, count

table.title: Retry Prevention and State Tracking Details

search.query: | tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ fillnull_value="n/a" count FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9819, 9820, 9821, 9822) AND host IN ($hostname$) BY _time, splunk_upgrade_events.date, splunk_upgrade_events.time, host, splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode, splunk_upgrade_events.status, splunk_upgrade_events.message, splunk_upgrade_events.details, splunk_upgrade_events.installer_name, splunk_upgrade_events.installer_hash, splunk_upgrade_events.target_version, splunk_upgrade_events.attempt_count, splunk_upgrade_events.last_status, splunk_upgrade_events.cleanup_status, splunk_upgrade_events.do_not_retry | sort 0 -_time | table splunk_upgrade_events.date, splunk_upgrade_events.time, host, splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode, splunk_upgrade_events.status, splunk_upgrade_events.message, splunk_upgrade_events.installer_name, splunk_upgrade_events.target_version, splunk_upgrade_events.attempt_count, splunk_upgrade_events.last_status, splunk_upgrade_events.cleanup_status, splunk_upgrade_events.do_not_retry, splunk_upgrade_events.details | rename splunk_upgrade_events.date AS Date, splunk_upgrade_events.time AS Time, host AS Hostname, splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode AS EventCode, splunk_upgrade_events.status AS Status, splunk_upgrade_events.message AS Message, splunk_upgrade_events.installer_name AS "Installer Name", splunk_upgrade_events.target_version AS "Target Version", splunk_upgrade_events.attempt_count AS "Attempt Count", splunk_upgrade_events.last_status AS "Last Status", splunk_upgrade_events.cleanup_status AS "Cleanup Status", splunk_upgrade_events.do_not_retry AS "Do Not Retry", splunk_upgrade_events.details AS Details

| tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ fillnull_value="n/a" count FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9820, 9830, 9831, 9832, 9833, 9834, 9835) AND host IN ($hostname$) BY _time, splunk_upgrade_events.date, splunk_upgrade_events.time, host, splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode, splunk_upgrade_events.status, splunk_upgrade_events.message, splunk_upgrade_events.details, splunk_upgrade_events.force_retry_file, splunk_upgrade_events.marker_file | sort 0 -_time | table splunk_upgrade_events.date, splunk_upgrade_events.time, host, splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode, splunk_upgrade_events.status, splunk_upgrade_events.message, splunk_upgrade_events.force_retry_file, splunk_upgrade_events.marker_file, splunk_upgrade_events.details | rename splunk_upgrade_events.date AS Date, splunk_upgrade_events.time AS Time, host AS Hostname, splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode AS EventCode, splunk_upgrade_events.status AS Status, splunk_upgrade_events.message AS Message, splunk_upgrade_events.force_retry_file AS "Force Retry File", splunk_upgrade_events.marker_file AS "Marker File", splunk_upgrade_events.details AS Details

| tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ fillnull_value="n/a" count FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9807, 9823) AND host IN ($hostname$) BY _time, splunk_upgrade_events.date, splunk_upgrade_events.time, host, splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode, splunk_upgrade_events.status, splunk_upgrade_events.message, splunk_upgrade_events.details | sort 0 -_time | table splunk_upgrade_events.date, splunk_upgrade_events.time, host, splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode, splunk_upgrade_events.status, splunk_upgrade_events.message, splunk_upgrade_events.details | rename splunk_upgrade_events.date AS Date, splunk_upgrade_events.time AS Time, host AS Hostname, splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode AS EventCode, splunk_upgrade_events.status AS Status, splunk_upgrade_events.message AS Message, splunk_upgrade_events.details AS Details

div.p: Raw structured upgrade events for detailed validation of event codes, status values, messages, helper activity, reset activity, and host-level execution behavior.

search.query: | tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE host IN ($hostname$) BY splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode | rename splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode AS eventcode | eval event_code_label=case( eventcode="9800", "9800 - No MSI installer detected in the splunk_installer directory.", eventcode="9801", "9801 - Splunk executable was not found. The script could not locate splunk.exe.", eventcode="9802", "9802 - Failed to create the Windows Scheduled Task.", eventcode="9803", "9803 - Windows Scheduled Task created successfully.", eventcode="9804", "9804 - Failed to remove the Windows Scheduled Task.", eventcode="9805", "9805 - Windows Scheduled Task started successfully.", eventcode="9806", "9806 - Windows Scheduled Task failed to start.", eventcode="9807", "9807 - Failed to remove the Splunk installer file from the splunk_installer directory.", eventcode="9808", "9808 - Splunkd service started successfully.", eventcode="9809", "9809 - Failed to start the Splunkd service.", eventcode="9810", "9810 - Splunkd service stopped successfully.", eventcode="9811", "9811 - Failed to stop the Splunkd service.", eventcode="9812", "9812 - Splunk Universal Forwarder upgrade completed successfully.", eventcode="9813", "9813 - Splunk Universal Forwarder upgrade failed.", eventcode="9814", "9814 - Failed to parse the currently installed Splunk Universal Forwarder version.", eventcode="9815", "9815 - Unable to extract the target version from the MSI installer filename.", eventcode="9816", "9816 - Target version is equal to or lower than the current version. Upgrade was skipped.", eventcode="9817", "9817 - Major version difference exceeds the supported upgrade path.", eventcode="9818", "9818 - Version validation checks passed.", eventcode="9819", "9819 - Installer already attempted; skipped to prevent retry loop.", eventcode="9820", "9820 - Force retry flag detected and removed; retry allowed.", eventcode="9821", "9821 - Upgrade state file written successfully.", eventcode="9822", "9822 - State file read/write/hash failure or force retry removal failure.", eventcode="9823", "9823 - Splunk installer file removed successfully.", eventcode="9830", "9830 - Force retry flag created by helper app.", eventcode="9831", "9831 - Force retry helper already ran; no new flag created.", eventcode="9832", "9832 - Force retry helper failed to create force retry flag.", eventcode="9833", "9833 - Force retry helper marker removed successfully.", eventcode="9834", "9834 - Force retry helper marker was not present; no reset needed.", eventcode="9835", "9835 - Failed to remove force retry helper marker.", true(), eventcode . " - Unknown event code" )

| tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ fillnull_value="n/a" count FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE host IN ($hostname$) AND splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN ($eventcode_token$) BY _time, host, splunk_upgrade_events.date, splunk_upgrade_events.time, splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode, splunk_upgrade_events.status, splunk_upgrade_events.message, splunk_upgrade_events.details, splunk_upgrade_events.previous_version, splunk_upgrade_events.current_version, splunk_upgrade_events.installer_name, splunk_upgrade_events.target_version, splunk_upgrade_events.attempt_count, splunk_upgrade_events.last_status, splunk_upgrade_events.cleanup_status, splunk_upgrade_events.do_not_retry, splunk_upgrade_events.force_retry_file, splunk_upgrade_events.marker_file | sort 0 -_time | table splunk_upgrade_events.date, splunk_upgrade_events.time, host, splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode, splunk_upgrade_events.status, splunk_upgrade_events.message, splunk_upgrade_events.details, splunk_upgrade_events.previous_version, splunk_upgrade_events.current_version, splunk_upgrade_events.installer_name, splunk_upgrade_events.target_version, splunk_upgrade_events.attempt_count, splunk_upgrade_events.last_status, splunk_upgrade_events.cleanup_status, splunk_upgrade_events.do_not_retry, splunk_upgrade_events.force_retry_file, splunk_upgrade_events.marker_file | rename splunk_upgrade_events.date AS Date, splunk_upgrade_events.time AS Time, host AS Hostname, splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode AS EventCode, splunk_upgrade_events.status AS Status, splunk_upgrade_events.message AS Message, splunk_upgrade_events.details AS Details, splunk_upgrade_events.previous_version AS "Previous UF Version", splunk_upgrade_events.current_version AS "Current UF Version", splunk_upgrade_events.installer_name AS "Installer Name", splunk_upgrade_events.target_version AS "Target Version", splunk_upgrade_events.attempt_count AS "Attempt Count", splunk_upgrade_events.last_status AS "Last Status", splunk_upgrade_events.cleanup_status AS "Cleanup Status", splunk_upgrade_events.do_not_retry AS "Do Not Retry", splunk_upgrade_events.force_retry_file AS "Force Retry File", splunk_upgrade_events.marker_file AS "Marker File"
